name: Boccone Dolce
recipeInstructions:
Recipe by: Vincent and Mary Price's "A Treasury of Reci
Preheat oven to 275°F. In a mixer, beat egg whites, salt, cream of tartar, vinegar, and vanilla until soft peaks form. Gradually beat in sugar and continue beating until meringue is stiff and glossy. Line baking sheets with parchment or waxed paper. Trace three, eight-inch circles on the paper. Spread meringue evenly and equally over the circles. Bake for one hour, until meringue becomes bisque colored. Turn off the oven and open the door; let meringues rest in the oven another 15 minutes. Remove from the oven and carefully peel off parchment or waxed paper. Place cake on racks to dry until thoroughly cool.
Once cooled, wrap meringues in saran and freeze. I wrap each meringue in several saran layers, then stack gently in a Tupperware container for freezing. Remove from freezer at least an hour before assembling.
Filling and Sauce: Melt chocolate pieces and water over hot water until smooth. Cool slightly but do not let harden. Whip heavy cream until stiff; gradually add confectioner's sugar and vanilla.
Slice two pints of strawberries: One is used between cake layers, the other is sliced, sugared, and refrigerated to produce more juice. The sugared berries are served as a sauce. Save several whole berries for garnish.
Assembly: Place a meringue layer on a serving plate with the rounded side down. Spread a very thin coating of chocolate over it, being careful not to use too much so the cake can be easily cut. Top with a layer (about 3/4-inch thick) of whipped cream. Top cream with a layer of sliced berries. Place a second meringue layer on top and repeat filling. Top with final meringue, rounded side up, and frost sides and top smoothly with remaining whipped cream. Additional cream may be used to pipe a design onto the sides and top of the cake.
Refrigerate at least four hours or overnight. Garnish with several whole strawberries and chocolate curls.
